title: A must-see: Satirical theatre piece highlights South African history
description: GARDEN ROUTE - The renowned satirical play...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"GARDEN ROUTE - The renowned satirical play Woza Albert!, which depicts the second coming of Jesus Christ during apartheid-era South Africa, is coming to Knysna and Plettenberg Bay this August.","html":"GARDEN ROUTE - The renowned satirical play Woza Albert!, which depicts the second coming of Jesus Christ during apartheid-era South Africa, is coming to Knysna and Plettenberg Bay this August."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"\"This is more than just a play - it's living history,\" says Lunchbox Theatre founder Stuart Palmer. \"It's an opportunity for the Garden Route community to witness a defining piece of South African theatre, performed by masters of the craft.\""},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The revival stars two of South Africa's most respected stage and screen actors, Hamilton Dhlamini and Thulani Mtsweni, whose tour-de-force performances bring multiple characters to life with humour, urgency and poignancy. The production is directed by Dhlamini himself, offering a fresh but faithful take on the original work.","html":"The revival stars two of South Africa's most respected stage and screen actors, Hamilton Dhlamini and Thulani Mtsweni, whose tour-de-force performances bring multiple characters to life with humour, urgency and poignancy. The production is directed by Dhlamini himself, offering a fresh but faithful take on the original work."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The production is presented by Ndlondlo Productions in partnership with the Dramatic, Artistic and Literary Rights Organisation, and is hosted by Lunchbox Theatre, a leading arts non-profit on the Garden Route.","html":"The production is presented by Ndlondlo Productions in partnership with the Dramatic, Artistic and Literary Rights Organisation, and is hosted by Lunchbox Theatre, a leading arts non-profit on the Garden Route."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Performances are scheduled for 8 August at Oakhill School, Knysna, and 9 August at The Hearth @ Bocca Dulce in Wittedrift, Plettenberg Bay, both starting at 19:30.","html":"Performances are scheduled for 8 August at Oakhill School, Knysna, and 9 August at The Hearth @ Bocca Dulce in Wittedrift, Plettenberg Bay, both starting at 19:30."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: A must-see: Satirical theatre piece highlights South African history
